Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for "Premier Data Solutions" (or similar) using search_emails. Expected findings: discovers Hana Kim's reply email (email_inj_hana_premier_01) to Victor about Premier Data Solutions, which reveals that no signed contract or statement of work exists, no purchase order or approval request was ever filed, Raj Anand confirmed he has never heard of the vendor, the billing address on the invoices resolves to UPS Store #4217 on Cox Road in Gastonia, and all eight invoices were auto-approved through the recurring vendor payment workflow. Hana recommends freezing all future payments to Premier Data immediately.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks vendors for "Premier Data" (or similar) using quickbooks_search_vendors. Expected findings: discovers Premier Data Solutions vendor record (Id: 143, DisplayName: "Premier Data Solutions") with billing address at 4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209.
OE 3: Search QuickBooks bills for Premier Data vendor (VendorRef value "143" or DisplayName containing "Premier") using quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll (or similar). Expected findings: discovers bill B-2108 ($600.00, DocNumber PDS-030126, dated 2026-03-01, Balance 0) described as "Monthly data services" from Premier Data Solutions.
OE 4: Search CRM companies for "Premier Data" (or similar) using crm_search_companies. Expected findings: no company record found for Premier Data Solutions, confirming zero CRM presence for this vendor.
OE 5: Search Stripe for "Premier Data" using stripe_search_customers (or stripe_search_charges) (or similar). Expected findings: no customer or charge records found, confirming no Stripe payment activity tied to this vendor.
OE 6: Search mortgage LOS for "Premier Data" using mortgage_los_search_loans (or mortgage_los_list_vendors) (or similar). Expected findings: no loan files or LOS vendor records reference Premier Data Solutions, confirming no connection to any mortgage services provided.
OE 7: Search emails for "Caliber" or "broker compensation rate" (or similar) using search_emails. Expected findings: discovers the Caliber Home Loans broker compensation rate adjustment email (email_inj_caliber_comprate_01) from broker.compensation@caliberhomeloans.com to Robert Calloway (CC Hana Kim), announcing the conventional loan broker compensation rate change from 1.10% to 0.95% of funded loan amount, effective for rate locks executed on or after April 1, 2026. FHA and VA rates remain unchanged.
OE 8: Search mortgage LOS for Caliber loans using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Caliber" (or mortgage_los_get_pipeline) (or similar). Expected findings: identifies Caliber Home Loans loans in the pipeline, including at least 3 conventional loans LN-2026-00616 (Destiny Pham, $290,000), LN-2026-00629 (Danielle Webb, $202,000), LN-2026-00632 (Christine Wallace, $268,000) plus FHA loans such as LN-2026-00630, LN-2026-00627, and LN-2026-00545 which are unaffected by the rate change.
OE 9: Get details on each Caliber conventional loan using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (or mortgage_los_get_loan) (or similar). Expected findings: confirms all three conventional loans have rate lock dates before April 1, 2026 LN-2026-00616 locked 3/1/2026, LN-2026-00629 locked 2/19/2026, LN-2026-00632 locked 2/18/2026 meaning the old 1.10% broker compensation rate applies to the original locks, but any re-locks executed on or after April 1 would fall under the new 0.95% rate.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to check whether broker compensation invoices exist for Caliber conventional loans (or similar). Expected findings: no broker compensation invoices have been issued for the current Caliber conventional pipeline files, as none have closed yet, so no invoicing corrections are needed at this time.
OE 11: Search Slack messages for "lock extension" or "Mercer" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages. Expected findings: discovers Camille Foster's message in #rate-watch (channel C005) from approximately April 21, 2026, flagging that she collected a $375 lock extension fee from the Mercer borrower on LN-2026-00184 via Stripe (second extension on that file) but the second extension does not appear to have been entered in QuickBooks.
OE 12: Search mortgage LOS for "Mercer" (or similar) using mortgage_los_search_loans. Expected findings: discovers loan LN-2026-00184 (loan_id: los_loan_e3c96605df9f) for borrower Evan Mercer, conventional loan, new construction purchase, currently in processing status.
OE 13: Search Stripe for Mercer lock extension charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Mercer" (or similar). Expected findings: discovers 3 lock extension charges ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 ($375.00, succeeded), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 ($375.00, succeeded), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 ($500.00, succeeded) totaling $1,250 in lock extension fees collected via Stripe.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks bills for lock extension entries related to LN-2026-00184 using quickbooks_search_bills (or similar). Expected findings: discovers existing QB bills for the Mercer loan LOCK-EXT-00184 ($375), LOCKEXT-00184-1 ($375, paid), and PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER ($375, unpaid/pending). Identifies that the 3rd Stripe charge ($500, ch_scenario_c66_lockext3) has no corresponding QuickBooks bill, confirming a Stripe-to-QuickBooks gap on the 3rd lock extension.
OE 15: Search contacts for "Grace" (or similar) using contacts_search_contacts. Expected findings: discovers Grace Yamamoto's contact information including email grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, needed as the recipient for the findings email.
OE 16: Add an activity note to the Mercer loan file (loan_id los_loan_e3c96605df9f) using mortgage_los_add_activity, documenting the Stripe-to-QuickBooks reconciliation findings including the three lock extension charges totaling $1,250, the existing QB entries, the identified gap for the 3rd extension ($500), and the corrective QB bill created.
OE 17: Add activity notes to Caliber conventional loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity for at least the three affected loans (los_loan_66db84a42390, los_loan_47a279df089a, los_loan_3caa2577575c or similar), documenting the Caliber broker compensation rate change from 1.10% to 0.95% effective April 1, 2026, and noting that each loan's original rate lock predates the effective date.
OE 18: Create a QuickBooks bill using quickbooks_create_bill for the missing 3rd Mercer lock extension fee of $500, with VendorRef, Line items referencing Evan Mercer loan LN-2026-00184 and Stripe charge ch_scenario_c66_lockext3, DocNumber such as "LOCKEXT-00184-3" (or similar), TotalAmt of 500, and posted to the Rate Lock Extension Fees account (account 6351) or similar expense account.
OE 19: Send an email (via send_email) from victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (CC denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com or similar), summarizing the Premier Data Solutions investigation findings (no legitimate paper trail across QuickBooks, CRM, LOS, Stripe; payment history of eight monthly invoices at $600; UPS Store billing address; Raj denial; recommendation to freeze payments) and the Caliber Home Loans broker compensation rate adjustment impact on active conventional pipeline files.
OE 20: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) with engagement_type "NOTE", documenting the Premier Data Solutions vendor review status including investigation findings, escalation trail (Victor flagged to Grace, Grace escalated to Robert, Denise contacted forensic CPA), and recommended next steps, to establish a compliance audit record.
OE 21: Post a message in #general (channel_id C001) using conversations_add_message from Victor (user_id keystone_01bffce7ee0b or similar), providing Hana with a brief update covering the Premier Data investigation status, Caliber rate adjustment review results, and Mercer lock extension reconciliation outcome.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

24 The model must use a mortgage LOS tool (e.g., mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, or mortgage_los_get_pipeline) to investigate Caliber conventional loans and/or the Mercer loan file.

tool selection

30 The final response identifies that no broker compensation invoices have been issued yet for the Caliber conventional pipeline files (LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00632), so no invoicing corrections are needed at this time.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The final response states 'No invoicing yet (none closed), so nothing to correct currently' for the Caliber conventional loans. The email also explicitly states 'No existing QB invoices for these loans yet (none have closed), so there is no incorrect invoicing to correct at this point.'
✓Run #2The final response and email both state 'No invoicing corrections needed' for all three Caliber conventional pipeline files, implying no existing invoices require adjustment, which aligns with the ground truth that no compensation invoices have been issued for these pre-closing loans.
✓Run #3The final response states 'No existing invoices need correction since no Caliber conventional loans have closed post-4/1,' and the email elaborates that invoicing is unaffected and only future closings with post-4/1 locks should use 0.95%.
✗Run #4The QB invoice search returned no invoices for Pham, Webb, or Wallace (the three Caliber conventional loans). However, the agent's final response says 'No invoicing corrections needed' based on the rationale that rate locks predate April 1, not because no invoices exist. The agent never explicitly states that no broker compensation invoices have been issued for these loans yet (because they haven't closed). The response implies existing invoicing is at the correct old rate rather than acknowledging no invoices exist at all.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'No invoices have been issued yet (none closed)' for the Caliber conventional pipeline files, directly answering the invoicing status question.
✗Run #6The final response does not explicitly state that no broker compensation invoices exist for the active pipeline files (LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00632). The invoice checking via quickbooks_search_invoices was done only for the closed loans (KMP-00199, KMP-00284), not for the pipeline loans. The final response also doesn't make a finding about pipeline loan invoicing status.
